acceptance_criteria: normalized_risk formula present in workbook cells and in plain-text in memo
CSV exports of key sheets included
One manual recomputation example present and values match workbook
Numeric values shown to at least two decimal places
expected_artifacts_after_revision: facilities_action.md (updated with formula block and manual recomputation)
verifier_warnings_to_address: openpyxl_unavailable - ensure workbook and CSV exports enable automated parsing
provisional_proxy_for_missing_limit - provide a sourced limit or clearly document the proxy

priority_items: reproducibility_missing_limit: Missing limit_chip_hotspot_C required a provisional proxy, reducing reproducibility.
xlsx_parsing_unavailable: Workbook could not be programmatically opened in the grader environment, preventing automated formula inspection.

planned_changes: Add limit_chip_hotspot_C to the embedded packet values or explicitly cite its source. If unavailable, choose and document a defensible proxy and label it 'provisional' in the packet, workbook, and memo.

In the workbook (liquid_cooling_stability.xlsx): place the normalized_risk calculation in a visible formula cell for each record, keep cached numeric values, and format displayed normalized_risk values to at least two decimal places.
Export the key sheets (Summary, Records, Checks) as CSV files and include them in the artifacts.zip so graders without openpyxl can parse numeric outputs.

In facilities_action.md: insert an exact plain-text formula block for normalized_risk, then show a one-record manual recomputation (inputs, limits, intermediate steps, final value rounded to two decimals).
Re-run all computations and sensitivity +/-5% checks, update memo tables to show recomputed values with two decimal places, and confirm that classifications are unchanged or document differences.
Update the memo's validation checks section to note that the workbook is openpyxl-compatible and that CSVs are provided for automated auditing.
Include a short 'changes vs baseline' bullet list in the memo summarizing all edits made for this revision.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Restore full reproducibility and satisfy automated grader guidance by providing the missing chip_hotspot_C limit (or documenting a justified proxy), embedding plain-text and workbook-visible normalized_risk formulas, ensuring workbook parseability (openpyxl), and adding a manual recomputation example with two-decimal numeric outputs.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission meets the artifact contract and presents plausible per-record normalized_risk values, classifications, sensitivity checks, and a defensible minimal-action recommendation. Two reproducibility/witnessability issues reduce full confidence: (1) the embedded packet omitted limit_chip_hotspot_C so a provisional proxy was used (this is flagged in the memo but prevents exact recomputation), and (2) the XLSX could not be programmatically opened in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), so automated formula inspection was not possible. Addressing these will restore full auditability and satisfy the evaluator guidance to preserve at least two decimal places when recomputing normalized_risk.
